name: Star Cooky Tree
recipeInstructions:
1-1/2 inch in diameter and 11 inches long. Candle, dowel or wooden stick may be used.
1. Make 2 recipes of Swedish Ginger cookie dough. Refrigerate.
2. Create star pattern. The bottom star (largest) measures 9 inches across at the points. Each subsequent cookie is 3/8 inch smaller than the last...for a total of 10 cookies. These are my observations, and it shows that if you make your first star, then gradually cut the pattern down by 3/8", this is how it's done.
3. Place pattern on dough which has been rolled to 1/4 inch thickness.
4. Cut around pattern with a knife. Make 10 stars of graduated sizes.
5. Cut a 3/4 inch hole in the center of each star.
6. Make six 2-1/2-inch, six 2-inch, and six 1-inch round cookies. (These go between the large stars on the pole.) Cut a 3/4 inch hole in the center of each.
7. Make another cookie star (smaller) for the top of the tree.
8. Bake as directed in the recipe. Cool.
9. Frost edges of stars with decorating frosting. Trim with colored candies. Allow frosting to dry.
10. Place center support in styrofoam base that is about 4-1/2 inches in diameter.
11. Assemble: Largest star, 2 largest round cookies, next large star, 2 round cookies, etc.
